ruleGroups.name: API6 Unrestricted Access To Sensitive Business Flows
API3 Broken Object Property Level Authorization

findings.name: Missing anti-automation signal
findings.description: Business flow endpoints should expose anti-automation controls.
findings.impact: Weak anti-automation protections can enable scripted abuse, enumeration, and repeated business workflow execution.
findings.remediation: Use rate limits, bot controls, and abuse monitoring for sensitive flows.

evidence.reason: Sensitive business endpoint has no visible anti-automation/rate-limit signal.
evidence.title: The endpoint did not present the expected anti-automation signal
evidence.summary: Sensitive business endpoint has no visible anti-automation/rate-limit signal.

findings.name: Admin path without auth
findings.impact: If confirmed, Admin path without auth can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Protect administrative functions with server-side authorization checks.

evidence.reason: Admin/function-level authorization probe returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success].
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Admin/function-level authorization probe returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success]. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Unsafe method override header
findings.description: Method override headers should not bypass authorization.
findings.impact: Method or function authorization gaps can let an attacker perform operations that should require higher privilege.
findings.remediation: Disable method override or apply authorization after effective method resolution.

evidence.reason: Request with unsafe method override returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success].
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with unsafe method override returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success]. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Privileged query flag
findings.description: Client-controlled privileged flags should be rejected.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Privileged query flag can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Ignore privileged client-supplied flags and derive privileges from identity.

evidence.reason: Request with privileged access tampering 'privileged-query-flag' returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success].
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with privileged access tampering 'privileged-query-flag' returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success]. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Role header tampering
findings.description: Role headers should not grant privileges.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Role header tampering can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Do not trust client-controlled role headers.

evidence.reason: Request with privileged access tampering 'role-header-admin' returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success].
evidence.title: Mutation triggered an unexpected application response
evidence.summary: Request with privileged access tampering 'role-header-admin' returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success]. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Missing rate-limit headers
findings.description: Sensitive APIs should expose standard or de-facto rate-limit signals.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Missing rate-limit headers can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Implement and communicate rate limits using standard RateLimit headers or documented compatibility headers.

evidence.reason: Baseline response does not expose common rate-limit headers.
evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
evidence.summary: Baseline response does not expose common rate-limit headers.

findings.name: Endpoint should reject missing Authorization header
findings.description: Authenticated endpoints should reject missing authorization.
findings.impact: An attacker may access protected functionality without valid authentication by removing an existing credential.

evidence.reason: Endpoint returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success] after auth credential was removed.
evidence.title: Endpoint accepted request after authentication was removed
evidence.summary: Endpoint returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success] after auth credential was removed. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Missing auth should not return same body as baseline
findings.description: Removing auth should not return the same sensitive body.
findings.impact: A missing credential boundary can allow a caller to continue using a session or token after it should have been rejected.
findings.remediation: Return an auth error body instead of protected resource data.

evidence.reason: Endpoint returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success] after auth credential was removed.
evidence.title: Endpoint accepted request after authentication was removed
evidence.summary: Endpoint returned HTTP 200 with success semantics [http_success] after auth credential was removed. Baseline status was 200 and mutated status was 200.

findings.name: Server version disclosure
findings.description: Server version headers should not disclose stack details.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Server version disclosure can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.

evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern

findings.name: Missing anti-sniffing security header
findings.description: APIs should prevent MIME sniffing where browser clients may consume responses.
findings.impact: If confirmed, Missing anti-sniffing security header can weaken the endpoint's security controls or expose sensitive operations and data.
findings.remediation: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on API responses. Use cache-control rules separately for sensitive data.

evidence.title: The baseline response matched a risky security pattern
